# Jarify Rule Reference

Complete list of built-in jarify rules. For style guidance and bad/good SQL
examples see [`sql-style-guide.md`](sql-style-guide.md).

## Understanding the table

| Column | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **Rule** | Kebab-case identifier. Use this in `-- jarify: disable-next-line <rule>` directives. |
| **Config key** | Key in `jarify.toml` under `[jarify]`. Both `snake_case` and `kebab-case` forms are accepted. |
| **Default** | `on` = always active; `off` = disabled; `warn` / `error` = active at that severity. |
| **Auto-fix** | `yes` = `jarify fmt` rewrites the violation automatically; `no` = lint only. |

## Rules

| Rule | Config key | Default | Auto-fix | Description |
|------|-----------|---------|---------|-------------|
| `keyword-case` | `uppercase_keywords` | on | yes | uppercase SQL keywords; lowercase type and scalar function names |
| `trailing-commas` | `trailing_commas` | off | yes | trailing comma placement (default: leading commas) |
| `no-implicit-cross-join` | `no_implicit_cross_join` | warn | yes | rewrite implicit cross joins to explicit CROSS JOIN |
| `no-select-star` | `no_select_star` | warn | no | flag SELECT * usage, including inside CTE bodies |
| `no-unused-cte` | `no_unused_cte` | warn | no | flag CTEs that are defined but never referenced |
| `duckdb-type-style` | `duckdb_type_style` | warn | no | prefer canonical DuckDB type names (e.g. int not integer) |
| `duckdb-prefer-qualify` | `duckdb_prefer_qualify` | warn | no | prefer QUALIFY clause over subquery window filter |
| `cte-naming` | `cte_naming` | warn | yes | CTE names must start with an underscore; fmt adds the prefix |
| `prefer-group-by-all` | `prefer_group_by_all` | warn | yes | rewrite explicit GROUP BY column list to GROUP BY ALL |
| `prefer-using-over-on` | `prefer_using_over_on` | warn | no | suggest USING (col) over ON a.col = b.col for equality joins |
| `consistent-empty-array` | `consistent_empty_array` | warn | yes | rewrite '[]'::type[] cast form to bare [] empty array literal |
| `prefer-neq-operator` | `prefer_neq_operator` | warn | yes | rewrite <> inequality operator to != |
| `prefer-if-over-case` | `prefer_if_over_case` | warn | yes | rewrite single-WHEN CASE expressions to IF() |
| `prefer-ifnull-over-coalesce` | `prefer_ifnull_over_coalesce` | warn | yes | rewrite two-argument COALESCE(x, y) to ifnull(x, y) |

## Configuring rules

Severity values: `"off"` Â· `"warn"` Â· `"error"`. Boolean flags accept `true` / `false`.

Both `snake_case` and `kebab-case` key forms are accepted in `jarify.toml`:

```toml
[jarify]
# snake_case (traditional)
no_select_star         = "error"
prefer-if-over-case    = "off"   # kebab-case also works

# silence a rule entirely
no_unused_cte          = "off"
```

## Disable directives

Suppress a rule for a single statement with an inline comment:

```sql
SELECT *  -- jarify: disable-next-line no-select-star
FROM my_table
;
```

Run `jarify rules` to print this table in your terminal, or
`jarify rules --format json` for a machine-readable list suitable for
editor integrations (e.g. LSP completion for disable directives).
